content.full_text:
TO: State Ethics Commissions

FROM: Gary R. Baker, Executive Director

DATE: April 24, 1991

I am writing this impersonal memorandum to once again to ask your assistance on some ethics issues we are facing.

We have been asked about the propriety of school personnel accepting gifts or prizes from vendors. The school personnel attend vendor exhibitions or conventions where door prizes are provided. Since there is a drawing for the door prize, the possibility of influencing any particular public employee is nil. The staff has drafted an opinion which advises that, if the door prize is of significant value, the employee who receives such a door prize not participate in procurements involving such vendor. Have you addressed the issue of door prizes from vendors? Are there any restrictions regarding acceptance or post-acceptance activities?

We are also involved in a matter involving a professional service organization (i.e., physicians practicing at a teaching institution). Have you addressed the issue of public employees who are not physicians participating in such professional service organization plans? How have you addressed such participation by physicians and other public employees in conjunction with any statute prohibiting receipt of additional money for carrying out the responsibilities of the public position?

If you could provide any opinions, decisions, court cases, etc. which may help us address these questions, it would be greatly appreciated if you send a response prior to May 8. Thanks for any help you can provide.
